title: Export
description: Export your data in different export formats.
exportingXlsx: Exporting XLSX. This may take a while.

annotations.dataDescription: All annotations, on all images in your current work folder, as a flat list in <w3cLink>W3C Web Annotation</w3cLink> JSON-LD format.
annotations.imagesDescription: All annotations, on all images in your current work folder, as an Excel file. Each top-level Entity Class will appear on a separate worksheet.
annotations.snippetsIntro: Image snippets can be included as a spreadsheet column:
annotations.snippetsBoundingBox: Bounding box snippets: rectangular images showing the full area around each annotation.
annotations.snippetsExactShape: Exact shape snippets: images clipped precisely to the annotated shape (applies to polygons and ellipses).
annotations.noSnippets: No snippets: export annotations without image snippets.

dataModel.entityClassesDescription: Your Entity Class model, in proprietary IMMARKUS JSON Format.
dataModel.relationshipTypesDescription: Your Relationship Type model, in proprietary IMMARKUS JSON Format.
dataModel.imageSchemasDescription: Your Image Metadata schemas, in proprietary IMMARKUS JSON Format.
dataModel.folderSchemasDescription: Your Folder Metadata schemas, in proprietary IMMARKUS JSON Format.
dataModel.fullModelDescription: All of the above - Entity Classes, Relationship Types, Image and Folder metadata schemas. This export is the same file you will find in your work folder as <fileName>_immarkus.model.json</fileName>. It is re-published here for convenience.

metadata.imageDescription: Image metadata, available in two versions: as a flattened CSV with one row per image and one column per metadata schema field, or an Excel spreadsheet. The Excel spreadsheet has separate worksheets for each image metadata schema.
metadata.folderDescription1: Folder metadata, available in two versions: as a flattened CSV with one row per folder and one column per metadata schema field, or an Excel spreadsheet.
metadata.folderDescription2: The Excel spreadsheet has separate worksheets for each folder metadata schema, and will also include IIIF manifest metadata, if any.

relationships.dataDescription: All relationships, on all images in your current work folder, as a flat list in <w3cLink>W3C Web Annotation</w3cLink> JSON-LD format.
relationships.imagesDescription: All relationships, on all images in your current work folder, as an Excel file. Image snippets for the start- and end-annotation of each relationship are included as spreadsheet columns.
